subject: Re: running WARP in Linux
body:
Hi Alister,

Thanks a lot! It takes a bit more time to get familiar. But we may install 
it on our cluster for much faster processing then.

Kind regards,
Yan

On Friday 11 October 2024 at 14:11:56 UTC+1 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Yan,
>
> Warp desktop will unfortunately not run on Linux, it was built with a UI 
> framework called windows presentation foundation and is windows specific.
>
> Instead, you now have access to the algorithms in Warp through a command 
> line tool called WarpTools - here is the documentation: 
> https://warpem.com/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/
>
> If you run into any issues please open an issue 
> https://github.com/warpem/warp
>
> Cheers and happy processing,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Oct 11, 2024, at 02:39, yah...@gmail.com <yah...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hello,
>
>
> I used to us the WARP 1.0 on Windows and now am switching to WARP 2.0 on 
> Ubuntu. I have followed the Github pape and installed WARP in a conda 
> environment. How shall I launch the graphic WARP viewer? Or does it run by 
> command lines and where to find a sheet of command lines?  Sorry for the 
> silly questions.
>
> Thanks a lot!
>
> Kind regards,
> Yan  
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.
> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an 
> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
> To view this discussion on the web visit 
> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/f777f4ad-2b2b-478e-a00a-6d4e02ca5526n%40googlegroups.com 
> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/f777f4ad-2b2b-478e-a00a-6d4e02ca5526n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
> .
>
>
